blockers.label: First-party advocate and legal-support sources
blockers.gap: Current advocate/legal targets are overwhelmingly quarantined COPAA-style directories and need replacement with first-party or official sources.

blockers.label: Exact forms libraries for most states
blockers.gap: Only 1 forms source is currently scrape-ready; most state form targets are blocked by fake-domain patterns or still too weak.

blockers.label: Repair generated fake official domains
blockers.gap: Many state office, DD, waiver, early intervention, and form targets are blocked because the repo currently has generated fake official domains.

blockers.label: More named first-party provider targets
blockers.gap: Provider coverage is still the biggest visible info gap even after the ready provider URLs are scraped.

blockers.label: Knowledge article source families
blockers.gap: The product needs many more explanatory content inputs, but the current target inventory is mostly directory/routing oriented.

blockers.label: Local providers, clinics, therapists, and care
blockers.gap: This is the biggest information hole if the goal is all local help, especially to compete on care and treatment discovery.

blockers.label: Advocates and legal/IEP support
blockers.gap: Advocate count is decent, but truth-safe local coverage is not yet strong enough to call exhaustive.

blockers.label: Findhelp-like metadata: availability, accessibility, intake, capacity
blockers.gap: The schema exists, but live high-signal metadata is sparse, especially on nonprofits and advocates.

blockers.label: Canonical org -> program -> location normalization
blockers.gap: Normalization exists, but service-location depth is still too thin to support a fully location-rich help product.

blockers.label: Conditions, functional needs, age bands, insurance types
blockers.gap: Conditions and needs are strong, but age-band and insurance reference tables are still empty.

blockers.label: Help content and explanatory knowledge
blockers.gap: The content layer is far too small for a product that aims to cover the full family journey nationally.

blockers.label: User submissions, coverage gaps, verification queue
blockers.gap: Operational feedback loops are not built out enough to sustain exhaustive maintenance.

surfaces.label: Family cases, child profiles, and case tracking

nextActions: Finish the remaining authored source families before broadening any scrape wave.
Burn down priority lightweight families in the existing completion plan before nonprofit overflow.
Keep runtime layers operational and seeded, but do not fabricate broad user-generated data.
Regenerate this artifact after each source-family unlock or runtime-surface implementation pass.
